evals.prompt: Read Docs/Specs/Inventory.md and create the implementation TODO list for it.
evals.expected_output: Creates Docs/Specs/Inventory-todo.md with a grouped Todo section only, includes Logic/UI/Data categorized tasks, and does not modify the source spec.
expectations: The skill reads the referenced spec before writing output.
The output file is written in the same folder as the spec using the <spec-stem>-todo.md naming pattern.
Todo items are grouped under task type or related feature headings.
Todo items use [Logic], [UI], or [Data] category tags.
The output does not include a Test cases section.
No implementation code or source spec changes are made.

evals.prompt: Turn the shop UI spec into todos. The spec is at Docs/Specs/Shop_UI.md.
evals.expected_output: Investigates existing shop/UI code and produces a same-folder TODO document that groups UI/UX interaction, empty/error/loading state, accessibility, and relevant logic/data tasks.
expectations: The skill performs codebase investigation before finalizing tasks.
The generated TODO includes UI/UX states required by the spec without inventing unrelated redesign work.
Tasks are grouped by task type or related feature area.
Tasks are split by responsibility instead of combining UI, logic, and data in one item.

evals.prompt: goal-todo for the latest spec in Docs/Specs; make the checklist actionable for implementation.
evals.expected_output: Finds likely spec candidates, asks for clarification if multiple plausible specs exist, then creates an actionable same-folder TODO checklist once the input spec is resolved.
expectations: The skill scans likely spec locations when no exact path is provided.
The skill asks for a specific spec when multiple plausible files exist.
The final checklist contains only grouped implementable tasks, not broad planning prose or test cases.
